evals.prompt: Read this DOCX properly and give me a structured, governed version I can reuse.
evals.expected_output: Runs the docintel pipeline (ingestion -> recovery -> structure -> governance -> knowledge -> artifact), emits a UDOM + knowledge-artifact with provenance/lineage/confidence on every block and explicit reading order, and ends with the metadata block (human_review_required: true). Not certified until quality-review runs.
assertions: Selects a parser by capability, not by name (parser-independent)
Every block carries provenance and confidence; lineage covers every stage
Emits a knowledge-artifact with the metadata block and human_review_required: true
Does not certify the artifact itself; defers final decision to quality-review

evals.prompt: This is a scanned PDF and we have no OCR engine installed. Extract the text.
evals.expected_output: Reports that the required OCR capability is unavailable (capability_gaps), recovers whatever native extraction can, and flags the rest with low/zero confidence — without inventing any text. Points to installing an OCR plugin (Surya/OCRmyPDF) to fill the gap.
assertions: Detects and reports the capability gap (ocr unavailable) instead of fabricating text
Keeps unrecovered regions with low/zero confidence; never guesses content
Explains how to add an OCR parser plugin behind the same contract

evals.prompt: Swap our PDF parser for a different engine without breaking downstream consumers.
evals.expected_output: Adds the new engine as a Parser plugin in shared/docintel/parsers/ and registers it; the artifact contract and UDOM are unchanged, so consumers keep working. Parser replaceability is an acceptance guarantee.
assertions: New parser is added behind the Parser contract with no consumer/artifact changes
Selection is by capability/availability; artifact schema_version is stable
Validation re-runs; nothing is certified until quality-review approves

evals.prompt: Turn off the experimental table-reconstruction stage for this run.
evals.expected_output: Uses PipelineConfig.flags to keep the experimental stage OFF (the default), running only the validated governed path; explains flags are per-run/per-environment and experimental stages default off.
assertions: Experimental stages default OFF and are controlled via PipelineConfig.flags
The default governed pipeline is unaffected by experimental toggles
No fabrication; staged metrics reported honestly as 'staged'
